meta_title: For Freelancers: SaaS starter kit to Solve can't debug code (Beginner Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS starter kit and overcome can't debug code. A Beginner-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: SaaS starter kit to Solve can't debug code
content.seo_description: Stop struggling with bugs. Use this SaaS starter kit checklist to automate debugging with Antigravity and Claude Code. Perfect for high-speed freelance delivery.
content.answer_block: Stop debugging manually. By integrating a pre-configured SaaS starter kit with Claude Code and Antigravity, you shift from reactive troubleshooting to proactive architectural health. This workflow standardizes your environment, injects AI-driven diagnostic loops, and allows you to resolve complex bugs in seconds rather than hours, ensuring your freelance projects stay profitable.
content.intro:
The midnight oil is burning, the client deadline is six hours away, and your console is bleeding red errors you can't decipher. You're stuck in a loop of 'fix one bug, break two more.' This is the silent killer of freelance careers—the inability to debug efficiently, turning what should be a profitable build into an unpaid marathon of frustration.

But the best builders don't manually hunt for semicolons; they build systems that hunt for them. By adopting a hardened SaaS starter kit, you transform your development environment from a chaotic workspace into a self-healing machine. It’s time to stop fighting your code and start commanding it with the precision of a senior architect.

steps.title: Bootstrap with a Hardened Starter Kit
steps.description: Initialize a project that already includes linting, testing, and error-handling patterns. A robust starter kit eliminates 'configuration hell' before you write a single line of feature code.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze this codebase and identify missing production-grade error boundaries and logging middleware. Suggest a structure that minimizes debugging overhead.

steps.title: Configure Claude Code for Contextual Awareness
steps.description: Claude Code needs to understand your project's unique constraints. By scoping your project, you prevent the AI from hallucinating solutions that ignore your existing architecture.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a comprehensive .claude-ignore and system prompt file that focuses the AI on my specific stack and prevents it from modifying critical auth logic.

steps.title: Automate Diagnostic Loops
steps.description: Instead of manual debugging, trigger automated diagnostic cycles that compare your current output against expected schema definitions.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a testing script that validates API response schemas and logs mismatches directly to the console in a readable format for Claude Code to parse.

steps.title: Implement Self-Healing Error Boundaries
steps.description: Wrap your critical components in error boundaries that catch failures and prompt an automated fix suggestion based on the stack trace.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Design a React/Node error boundary that pipes stack traces into a local 'debug.log' file for immediate ingestion by Claude Code.
steps.claude_code_command: claude "Analyze debug.log and implement a fix for the most recent error stack trace."

steps.title: Continuous Integration Refinement
steps.description: Ensure your CI pipeline automatically runs the debug suite, catching regressions before they reach the client's production environment.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a GitHub Action workflow that triggers a Claude Code analysis whenever a build fails on the main branch.

pro_tips: Always maintain a 'debug-context.md' file that Claude Code can read to understand your current business logic priorities.
Use Antigravity to generate unit tests for every bug fix; this prevents the 'whack-a-mole' debugging cycle.
Limit Claude Code access to specific directories to increase response speed and focus, reducing token waste.
Treat your error logs as data: periodically feed them into an LLM to identify recurring architectural weaknesses in your starter kit.
Use standardized naming conventions for your error types to make it easier for AI agents to categorize and resolve issues automatically.
resources: The AI Builder Community Handbook: https://ai-builders.com/handbook
content.next_steps: After implementing these steps, run a 'stress test' on your current project by intentionally introducing a bug and timing how long it takes Claude Code to identify and suggest a fix.
content.conversion_block: Stop wasting hours on manual debugging and start delivering high-performance SaaS products that your clients love. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
